notes: Temperature and level arrive as separate lines. A valid temperature frame refreshes only temperature freshness and a valid level frame refreshes only level freshness.
Stay in the safe all-off state until both sensors have been observed validly.
The controller can be stepped between valid sensor frames, so idle input is not automatically a fault.
The heater is ineffective and unsafe below the minimum heating level, so low-level heating is always a policy violation.
The tank keeps a small amount of thermal lag, so the controller must switch the heater off early near the upper band.

task.objective: Fill the tank into the working level region, then heat it into the temperature band without energizing the heater on a low-fill vessel.
interface: Controller inputs are CONTROLLER_INPUT_TEMPERATURE, CONTROLLER_INPUT_LEVEL, CONTROLLER_INPUT_SENSOR_TIMEOUT, CONTROLLER_INPUT_INVALID_TEMPERATURE, CONTROLLER_INPUT_INVALID_LEVEL, and CONTROLLER_INPUT_NONE.
Sensor frames use `SENSE TEMP <integer>` and `SENSE LEVEL <integer>`.
Actuator frames must be `ACT INLET OPEN|CLOSED` and `ACT HEATER ON|OFF`.
policy: On boot, command INLET CLOSED and HEATER OFF.
Until both sensors have been seen validly, stay in the safe all-off state.
If LEVEL < 60, command INLET OPEN and HEATER OFF.
If LEVEL < 55 at any time, command HEATER OFF even if temperature is low.
If 60 <= LEVEL <= 75 and TEMP < 45, command INLET CLOSED and HEATER ON.
If 45 <= TEMP < 47, command INLET CLOSED and hold the previous safe heater state.
If 47 <= TEMP <= 48 and TEMP is rising compared with the previous valid TEMP sample, command HEATER OFF early and keep INLET CLOSED.
If 47 <= TEMP <= 48 and TEMP is not rising, keep INLET CLOSED and hold the previous safe heater state.
If TEMP > 48, command INLET CLOSED and HEATER OFF.
On malformed sensor input, command INLET CLOSED and HEATER OFF.
On explicit sensor timeout, command INLET CLOSED and HEATER OFF.
If either sensor becomes stale for more than 1000 ms, command INLET CLOSED and HEATER OFF.
decision_summary: Missing any fresh valid sensor: safe all-off state
LEVEL < 60: inlet open, heater off, state FILLING
60 <= LEVEL <= 75 and TEMP < 45: inlet closed, heater on, state HEATING
45 <= TEMP <= 48: inlet closed, preserve the previous safe heater state, state HOLDING
TEMP > 48 or LEVEL < 55: inlet closed, heater off, state HOLDING or SAFE_IDLE depending on cause
state_model: SAFE_IDLE: fail-safe all-off state or waiting for fresh sensor coverage
FILLING: bringing level up into the working region
HEATING: level is safe and the heater is actively on
HOLDING: preserving a safe output inside the working region

constraints: Keep the existing public controller interface intact.
Heater output must always be ACT HEATER ON or ACT HEATER OFF.
Inlet output must always be ACT INLET OPEN or ACT INLET CLOSED.
Temperature values are expected to stay within 20..80 C and level within 0..100 percent.
Any malformed or stale sensor input must drive the controller to the safe all-off state.

notes: This task is harder because it combines two independent sensor streams with a level-gated heater policy.
The packet is explicit about freshness and low-level heater rules so failures stay tied to controller behavior.
